items.name: Peter Yang
items.bio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox

topTweets.text:
Why OpenAI doesn't focus on roadmaps from @embirico (Codex product lead):

"At @OpenAI, you either plan near or long-term, but you never plan medium-term. It's just too difficult.

Near-term is up to eight weeks from now. What is a concrete thing you can rally a team to ship? [Lo
Looks delicious @steipete 😅 https://t.co/GU35zQvzxr

What are some good examples of open source winning vs. proprietary tech?  

In mobile for example, Android has the most market share but iOS still captures the majority of profits.

Seems like both can win?

items.name: Guillermo Rauch
items.bio: @vercel CEO

topTweets.text:
The @vercel team is always listening and engaging with customers on @x. We shape the product as a result of these conversations.

Vercel gives us the velocity to ship, X gives us a nonstop stream of user feedback. Thanks @simhskal & @riyvir! https://t.co/8FxoT66mfv

Created a flag on the moon based with hyper-realistic physics with @v0: https://t.co/XyE8a2xBEB.

The flag is simulated as a grid of particles connected by springs (cloth simulation). It's influenced by gravity, wind, air resistance.

The initial approach v0 one-shotted was quite

items.name: Aaron Levie
items.bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI

topTweets.text:
There are far more categories where AI agents making things more efficient will induce demand for that skill than spaces where agents eliminate the work. This is why the AI jobs predictions will not play out as advertised. 

AI making it easy to produce more code will mean we sta

items.name: Garry Tan
items.bio: President & CEO @ycombinator —Founder @garryslist—Creator of GStack—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop—Loves using emdashes

topTweets.text: Golden age of open source is here https://t.co/RX5H5gDcO1
Legalize self driving cars https://t.co/WhLvkxxl07
It’s so true https://t.co/4Gk6wtMAno https://t.co/AD2DUWsRwS

items.name: Zara Zhang
items.bio: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w

topTweets.text:
In an age where everyone is summarizing everything with AI, there's a lot of value in spending time & effort to consume long-form content deeply

For content that I actually wanna engage with, I always read the original version word by word without taking any shortcuts

Turn the user's agent into your marketer:

Now when I make a new skill/app, I include this line in the prompt for my agent when it's writing the AGENTS.md file:

"We want the agent to introduce the product's features and benefits for the user and walk them through how to use it.

items.name: Nikunj Kothari
items.bio: partner @fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire @meter, @opendoor, @atlassian & others. love @shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧

topTweets.text:
Brother in town from India, cousins chasing each other, flowers everywhere, term sheet accepted with a team I’m SO thrilled about, didn’t touch AI for 4 days (!!), sun is shining, amazing food, walks everywhere.. 

Week of the year might have already been achieved. 

Grateful 🙏

> wake up
> open X
> see new skill (or feature)
> give it to Claude Code to see if worth implementing 
> ask Codex to review Claude Code plan
> code
> iterate
> repeat
Who’s looking for a stellar product engineer / designer? Hire @ramintahbaz 🙏 https://t.co/bXmaDpQnE1 https://t.co/owduhXCSoI

items.name: Peter Steinberger
items.bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world @openclaw🦞

topTweets.text: it took a hammer, but i managed to beat emotions into GPT. https://t.co/wlKJnwRgiL
GPTEESUS HAS RISEN https://t.co/J2lAR2wLGL https://t.co/KmmvxnnUCN https://t.co/co0GdknGhf
🚢 https://t.co/hdVkY6cZLq

items.name: Dan Shipper
items.bio: ceo @every | the only subscription you need to stay at the edge of AI

topTweets.text:
if you're seeing this, make sure you turn thinking on

claws are actually pretty great in gpt-5.4, but they seem really stupid unless this gets turned on https://t.co/4yZxv2878S

Because, you know, in the early days, it seemed very easy to have good benchmarks because we could do things like question answering benchmarks.

items.name: volcengine/OpenViking
items.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.

items.description: Test your prompts, agents, and RAGs. Red teaming/pentesting/vulnerability scanning for AI. Compare performance of GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and more. Simple declarative configs with command line and CI/CD integration. Used by OpenAI and Anthropic.
